source: Joint Deep Modeling of Users and Items Using Reviews for Recommendation

concepts.finding: Joint deep model: users and items both represented via stacked denoising autoencoders on their review texts. User review text encodes preferences; item review text encodes features. PMF connects user and item deep representations for rating prediction. Outperforms PMF and CDL on rating prediction.
concepts.implication: Keyword embedding from document context: the documents/pages where a keyword appears encode its semantics. Stacked autoencoder on keyword-document matrix captures document-level context for keyword intent.
concepts.open_question: Should keyword embedding use the text of the keyword itself, the documents containing it, or both?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Joint deep model combining keyword text (autoencoder) with document context (co-occurrence matrix) produces more accurate intent clusters than either signal alone.
